theory.intro: Partitioning splits a number into parts. The standard partition uses place value; non-standard partitions regroup those parts in other useful ways.
facts: Renaming counts a number in one unit, e.g. $4\,300\,000 = 43$ hundred-thousands.
steps: For a standard partition, write each digit times its place.
To regroup, take $1$ from a higher place and add its value ($10$ of the next unit) lower down.
